assessments.status_rationale: Today’s items discuss escalation in the Middle East/Iran conflict and oil price impacts, but none cite a Lloyd’s Market Association Joint War Committee public listed-areas update/circular adding or materially expanding a war-risk listed area for the Strait of Hormuz and/or Gulf of Oman.

The news notes oil price surges and war escalation, but provides no broker/index assessment (Clarksons/Baltic Exchange/Platts) reporting Middle East–linked VLCC spot earnings at or above $150,000/day on a specific day.
No cited marine insurance broker client note (Marsh/Howden/Aon/Gallagher/Willis) is included stating war-risk additional premium for a single Strait of Hormuz transit reached ≥0.50% of hull value.
There is no evidence in today’s bullets about a Federal Reserve decision changing the federal funds target range relative to 2026-03-29 levels; the prediction’s deadline is later in 2026.

Although gas prices and travel behavior are discussed, nothing indicates a major U.S. passenger airline has publicly implemented a temporary passenger-ticket fuel/energy surcharge via press release, investor update, or fare-rule filing.
No reporting here mentions the Bank of Russia making a single scheduled 100 bps (or larger) key-rate hike.

One bullet references South Korean markets, but none mention South Korea publishing an official implementation measure (decree/guideline/standard) for a public-parking-lot solar mandate specifying timing and covered categories.

A CNBC item mentions Meta court losses and AI research/safety issues, but there is no announcement of production deployment of an Arm-based custom general-purpose data-center CPU running in Meta data centers.

Opinion/analysis about chatbots and therapy appears, but there is no European Commission announcement opening a formal DSA proceeding (or equivalent) against an AI chatbot/companion service citing risks to minors and/or mental-health harms.
